findings.evidence: Grep shows `toEquiv_one` at lines 106-107 and `toEquiv_mul` at lines 109-111; `rg "toEquiv_inv" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Diffeomorphism/Group.lean` has no hit, while Mathlib already has `@[simp] Diffeomorph.symm_toEquiv` for the underlying inverse.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp] theorem toEquiv_inv (f : M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M) : (f⁻¹).toEquiv = f.toEquiv⁻¹ := rfl` (or the equivalent `f.toEquiv.symm` form).
findings.issue: The file exposes `@[simp] toEquiv_one` and `@[simp] toEquiv_mul`, but no corresponding `toEquiv_inv`, so the underlying-permutation API does not characterize all group operations.

summary: The group API now covers inverse at the function level, but the exposed `toEquiv_*` characteristic API is still incomplete for the inverse operation. Add the missing simp lemma so downstream code can use the forgetful equivalence API without unfolding the group instance.
